title: MDISCO: A High-Throughput Tissue-Clearing Protocol for Mouse Brains
author: Madeline Martinez
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML
materials: Double-distilled H 2 O (laboratory supply) Laboratory supplies
Paraformaldehyde (PFA) powder, EM-grade (Fisher Scientific, catalog number: AC
PBS powder, pH 7.4 (Sigma-Aldrich, catalog number: P
Hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 ) solution, 30% (Thermo Scientific, catalog number: 033323.AY)
Dibenzyl ether (DBE), ≥99% (Thermo Scientific, catalog number: A18447.
Ethyl cinnamate (ECi), ≥98% (Thermo Scientific, catalog number: A12906.
Glass scintillation vials (e.g., Grainger, catalog number: 3LDT2)

steps.title: B6
steps.instruction: B6.Cg- Gt(ROSA)26Sor tm9(CAG-tdTomato)Hze /J mice [Jackson Laboratory, strain #007909; common name: Ai9 or Ai9(RCL-tdT)] [7]

steps.title: STOCK Fos tm2
steps.instruction: STOCK Fos tm2.1(icre/ERT2)Luo /J mice [Jackson Laboratory, strain #030323, common name: Fos 2A-iCreER (TRAP2)] [8]

steps.title: TRAP2/Ai9 mice [TRAP2 ( Fos 2A-iCreER ) and Rosa26LSL-tdTomato (Ai
steps.instruction: TRAP2/Ai9 mice [TRAP2 ( Fos 2A-iCreER ) and Rosa26LSL-tdTomato (Ai

steps.title: B6
steps.instruction: B6.129(Cg)- Oprl1 tm1.1Mrbr /J mice (gifted from the Bruchas Lab at the University of Washington, Jackson Laboratory, strain #036308, common name: NOPR loxP/YFP ) [10] Reagents

steps.title: Anesthetize the mouse according to approved institutional animal care protocols
steps.instruction: Anesthetize the mouse according to approved institutional animal care protocols.

steps.title: Perfuse transcardially with 20–30 mL of...
steps.instruction: Perfuse transcardially with 20–30 mL of 1× PBS at a flow rate of approximately 5–10 mL/min until the liver clears.

steps.title: Immediately perfuse with 20–30 mL of...
steps.instruction: Immediately perfuse with 20–30 mL of freshly prepared 4% (w/v) PFA in PBS at the same flow rate. PFA powder is dissolved in 1× PBS at 60 °C with 1 M NaOH added dropwise to facilitate dissolution; pH is adjusted to 7.

steps.title: The solution is cooled and filtered through a 0
steps.instruction: The solution is cooled and filtered through a 0.45 μm filter before use.

steps.title: Dissect the brain carefully and immerse...
steps.instruction: Dissect the brain carefully and immerse it in 10–15 mL of 4% PFA to ensure complete submersion.

steps.title: Fix overnight at 4 °C with gentle agitation if possible
steps.instruction: Fix overnight at 4 °C with gentle agitation if possible. Day

steps.title: Transfer the fixed brain into a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the fixed brain into a clean container containing 1× PBS.

steps.title: Incubate overnight at 37 °C with rocking if possible
steps.instruction: Incubate overnight at 37 °C with rocking if possible. Day

steps.title: Inspect the sample and ensure all...
steps.instruction: Inspect the sample and ensure all hair or debris is removed from the brain surface.

steps.title: Transfer the brain sequentially through the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the brain sequentially through the following MeOH/water (v/v) gradients, each for 1 h at RT, enough to fill the vial of your choice completely: 20% MeOH; 40% MeOH; 60% MeOH; 80% MeOH. Note: All solvent concentrations and mixture ratios in this protocol are defined as (v/v) unless otherwise stated.

steps.title: Incubate the brain in 100% MeOH...
steps.instruction: Incubate the brain in 100% MeOH for 1 h at RT.

steps.title: Replace with fresh 100% MeOH and incubate overnight at RT
steps.instruction: Replace with fresh 100% MeOH and incubate overnight at RT. Pause point: The samples can be stored in 100% MeOH (see General note 3). Day

steps.title: Transfer the brain to 66% DCM/33% MeOH. Caution
steps.instruction: Transfer the brain to 66% DCM/33% MeOH. Caution: Many organic solvents soften many plastics; use only solvent-resistant containers.

steps.title: Incubate overnight at RT with rocking
steps.instruction: Incubate overnight at RT with rocking. Day

steps.title: Wash the brain in 100% MeOH...
steps.instruction: Wash the brain in 100% MeOH for 2 h at RT.

steps.title: Replace with fresh 100% MeOH and...
steps.instruction: Replace with fresh 100% MeOH and incubate an additional 2 h at RT.

steps.title: Prepare 6% H 2 O 2...
steps.instruction: Prepare 6% H 2 O 2 in MeOH fresh just before use (1 part 30% H 2 O 2 : 4 parts MeOH).

steps.title: Incubate the brain in 6% H...
steps.instruction: Incubate the brain in 6% H 2 O 2 /MeOH overnight at 4 °C. Critical: Always prepare fresh 6% H 2 O 2 /MeOH for bleaching; old peroxide increases background. Day

steps.title: Wash the brain in 100% MeOH...
steps.instruction: Wash the brain in 100% MeOH for 1 h at RT.

steps.title: Replace with fresh 100% MeOH and incubate overnight at RT
steps.instruction: Replace with fresh 100% MeOH and incubate overnight at RT. Day

steps.title: Incubate the brain in 66% DCM/33%...
steps.instruction: Incubate the brain in 66% DCM/33% MeOH for 3 h at RT.

steps.title: Replace the solution with 100% DCM...
steps.instruction: Replace the solution with 100% DCM and incubate for 1 h at RT.

steps.title: Transfer the brain to 100% DBE
steps.instruction: Transfer the brain to 100% DBE.

steps.title: Rock at RT until the tissue...
steps.instruction: Rock at RT until the tissue becomes cleared (typically several hours). Final transfer to imaging medium (ECi)

steps.title: Keep the brain in DBE for...
steps.instruction: Keep the brain in DBE for 4–5 days, allowing internal bubbles to dissipate fully. Optional: Place in a 37 °C incubator, checking hourly, to accelerate bubble removal.

steps.title: Once bubbles are eliminated and the...
steps.instruction: Once bubbles are eliminated and the tissue appears optically uniform, replace the solution with ECi for final RI matching and storage. Final morphological measurements are taken after this stage ( Figure 1 ). Figure

steps.title: Brain morphology and transparency across processing stages
steps.instruction: Brain morphology and transparency across processing stages. (A) Representative dorsal views of whole mouse brains at baseline (PBS) and after refractive index matching in ethyl cinnamate (ECi) following processing with MDISCO or FDISCO+. Scale bar, 1 cm. (B–E) Quantification of percent change relative to PBS baseline in brain weight (B), light transmittance (C), mediolateral (ML) width (D), and anteroposterior (AP) length (E) after clearing. Each dot represents an individual brain; horizontal lines indicate mean ± SEM. Statistical comparisons between MDISCO and FDISCO+ were performed using unpaired t tests with Welch’s correction. **p < 0.01, ****p < 0.0001; ns, not significant.

description: Validation of protocol To validate the performance and reproducibility of the MDISCO clearing protocol, tissue morphology, optical transparency, and endogenous fluorescence preservation were quantitatively compared with FDISCO+ [6]. As shown in Figure 1 , both clearing methods produced measurable changes in brain morphology relative to the PBS baseline. MDISCO- and FDISCO+-cleared brains exhibited comparable changes in anteroposterior (AP) length, with no statistically significant difference between methods. In contrast, mediolateral (ML) shrinkage was significantly reduced in MDISCO-cleared samples relative to FDISCO+, indicating improved preservation of lateral tissue dimensions. Brain weight changes followed a similar trend, with MDISCO showing reduced variability across samples. Consistent with prior reports indicating that peroxide formation in DBE at room temperature contributes to endogenous fluorescence decay, prolonged exposure to DBE was avoided in this protocol [6]. Optical transparency, quantified as normalized light transmittance, was significantly increased in MDISCO-cleared brains compared to FDISCO+, reflecting more uniform refractive index matching and reduced residual scattering. Although the FDISCO+ protocol was implemented repeatedly with careful pH control, recommended antioxidant additives, and early transfer to ECi, its performance was variable and consistently inferior to MDISCO under our experimental conditions, highlighting the sensitivity of THF-based workflows to experimental context. Fluorescence preservation was evaluated to assess the robustness and reproducibility of the MDISCO clearing protocol. All images were acquired using a Miltenyi UltraMicroscope Blaze II light-sheet microscope with identical acquisition settings applied across samples within each fluorophore group to control for imaging-related variability. Imaging was performed using 60% laser power, a step size of 3 μm, a light-sheet numerical aperture (NA) of 3.9 μm, and a
